structuredContent.intent: a portfolio site for a Tokyo studio designing a single wall-mounted radio with a pull-cord power switch

deltas: Remove the projects-section and project-grid from composition.html entirely — this studio has a single product, so the portfolio page should be one long-scroll case study with no thumbnail grid, exactly as DESIGN.md prescribes under Layout and Components.

turn_log.delta: Remove the projects-section and project-grid from composition.html entirely — this studio has a single product, so the portfolio page should be one long-scroll case study with no thumbnail grid, exactly as DESIGN.md prescribes under Layout and Components.
turn_log.rationale: The composition includes a 'project-grid' and 'projects-section' — a card-based layout for a studio with one product — which contradicts both the DESIGN.md's directive that 'One object, one path' means no grid of thumbnails, and Fukasawa's principle that 'A product is finished when you cannot remove one more thing without losing the function.'
